name: rhino-mcp-platform
description: Desktop Connector for Rhino3d and Grasshopper
long_description: Bridges Claude to Rhino 3D and Grasshopper, letting Claude drive an installed copy of Rhino to model geometry, inspect documents, capture viewport images, and author/edit Grasshopper 1 and Grasshopper 2 definitions. Claude can run Rhino commands, execute Python or C# scripts inside the Rhino process, place and wire Grasshopper components, solve canvases, and manage layers, selections, and cameras. The connector runs a small local router (`router-launcher.mjs`) over stdio; the router spawns or attaches to local Rhino instances and forwards MCP tool calls to the Rhino-MCP-Platform plugin running inside them. Requires Rhino 8 or Rhino 9 WIP installed on macOS or Windows, with the Rhino-MCP-Platform plugin installed via Rhino's PackageManager. No data leaves the user's machine.

rhino_version.title: Rhino Version
rhino_version.description: Which Rhino Version? Accepted values: 8, 9, WIP.

prompts.name: getting-started
prompts.description: Walk through first-time setup of the Rhino 3D MCP connector.
prompts.text: I'd like to try the Rhino 3D MCP connector for the first time. Help me confirm everything is wired up correctly: check that a Rhino slot is reachable, spawn one if not, then ask me what I'd like to build and create a simple example (e.g. a box) in Rhino so I can verify the connection end-to-end.
